channel.title: Nature Physics
channel.description: Nature Physics offers news and reviews alongside top-quality research papers in a monthly publication, covering the entire spectrum of physics. Physics addresses the properties and interactions of matter and energy, and plays a key role in the development of a broad range of technologies. To reflect this, Nature Physics covers all areas of pure and applied physics research. The journal focuses on core physics disciplines, but is also open to a broad range of topics whose central theme falls within the bounds of physics.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Publisher Correction: Driving Floquet physics with excitonic fields
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 31 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03264-w

Publisher Correction: Driving Floquet physics with excitonic fields
item.dc:title: Publisher Correction: Driving Floquet physics with excitonic fields
item.dc:creator: Vivek Pareek
David R. Bacon

Marcos G. Menezes

Joel Pérez Urquizo

Julien Madéo
Gianluca Stefanucci
Diana Y. Qiu
Kuan Eng Johnson Goh
Felipe H. da Jornada
Keshav M. Dani

item.title: Topology uncovered in real space
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 31 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03199-2

There are theoretical predictions that topologically non-trivial states in materials leave tell-tale signs in the spatial structure of their wave functions. These have now been observed in monolayer materials.
item.dc:title: Topology uncovered in real space
item.dc:creator: Sathwik Bharadwaj

item.title: Observation of giant nonlinear valley Hall effect
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 31 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03221-7

Previous observations of the valley Hall effect have been limited to the linear regime. Now a nonlinear version is demonstrated with a larger magnitude than in the linear case.
item.dc:title: Observation of giant nonlinear valley Hall effect
item.dc:creator: Pan He
Min Zhang
Jin Cao
Jingru Li
Hao Liu
Jinfeng Zhai
Ruibo Wang
Cong Xiao
Shengyuan A. Yang
Jian Shen

item.title: Observation of an obstructed atomic band in a transition metal dichalcogenide
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 31 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03196-5

Atomic insulators come in two varieties: so-called unobstructed and obstructed types. The former are common and now scanning tunnelling microscopy experiments provide evidence for the latter.
item.dc:title: Observation of an obstructed atomic band in a transition metal dichalcogenide
item.dc:creator: Dumitru Călugăru
Yi Jiang
Haojie Guo
Sandra Sajan
Yongsong Wang
Haoyu Hu
Jiabin Yu
B. Andrei Bernevig
Fernando de Juan
Miguel M. Ugeda

item.title: A tunable topological photonic interferometer
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 30 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03227-1

Robust interference between photonic topological edge states, without compromising unidirectional transmission, is achieved. Optical gain enables fast, reconfigurable control of mode coupling, thus realizing a tunable on-chip topological interferometer.
item.dc:title: A tunable topological photonic interferometer
item.dc:creator: Yandong Li
